label: Transparent salt marsh motif
prompt:
Use case: botanical-natural
Asset type: Morphous transparent motif cutout source
Primary request: Create a dense sculptural tuft of salt marsh grass, isolated full subject, no surrounding landscape, no props, no text.
Subject: coastal wetland cordgrass with long blade clusters, seed heads, brackish-weathered straw tips, tiny silver water glints on blades, and a compact dark wet mud base. The motif should read as salt marsh grass, not lawn grass or a houseplant.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; full tuft visible from mud base to blade tips; crisp silhouette suitable for a catalog motif asset.
Style: refined natural-history botanical illustration with realistic texture and clean edges.
Palette: marsh green blades, reed olive shadows, pale straw tips, silver water highlights, dark brown-gray mud.
Transparent workflow: place the subject on a perfectly flat solid #ff00ff chroma-key background for removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no text, no watermark, no pot, no vase, no flowers, no insects, no animals, no horizon, no landscape, no cast shadow, no reflection.

label: Light design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous light-mode design-system board for Salt Marsh
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a motif-driven UI theme named "Salt Marsh", derived from coastal wetland cordgrass, tidal channels, silver water, dark mud flats, and green marsh blades.
Canvas: wide 16:9 high-resolution board intended final use at 3840x2160. Use a precise product-design grid with large readable labels and enough whitespace for gallery preview downscaling.
System feel: tidal logistics system. The UI should feel like calm operational software for wetland routing, dock windows, field teams, water-level monitoring, task dispatch, and inventory flow.
Mood and UI language: dense but orderly logistics dashboard, quiet coastal utility, vertical grass-blade dividers, meandering tidal-channel lines, mudflat texture strips, 8px radius cards, crisp shadcn/tweakcn-style controls, restrained shadows, scan-friendly tables.
Required visible sections: title "Salt Marsh", motif story, palette swatches, typography with English and Japanese examples, compact left navigation, top navigation, route/tide timeline, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, map/channel panel, data table, form fields, search/command bar, tabs, buttons, badges, alerts, checkbox/radio/switch controls, loading/empty/error status states, spacing/radius/shadow tokens, CSS token snippets, texture strip, generated asset examples.
Palette: pale silver-water background, mist shell cards, marsh green primary, deep channel green foreground, reed olive secondary, mud brown muted surfaces, silt gray borders, tidal blue-green accent, amber wrack warning, clay red destructive only for errors.
Text: short readable UI labels only; no long paragraphs. Use generic product UI labels such as Tide Ops, Routes, Berths, Crews, Water Level, Inventory, Dispatch, Checkpoint, Forms, Table, Analytics, Search, Ready, Pending, Alert.
Constraints: no watermark, no random brand names, no unrelated bright colors, no purple gradients, no neon green, no illegible microtext, no marketing hero layout, no animal imagery.

label: Dark design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous dark-mode design-system board for Salt Marsh
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive dark-mode version of the Salt Marsh design-system board. It must feel like the same coastal wetland tidal logistics system translated to dark mode, not a new theme.
Canvas: wide 16:9 high-resolution board intended final use at 3840x2160. Use a precise product-design grid with large readable labels and enough spacing for gallery preview downscaling.
System feel: tidal logistics system. The UI should feel like calm operational software for wetland routing, dock windows, field teams, water-level monitoring, task dispatch, and inventory flow.
Mood and UI language: nocturnal marsh operations dashboard, deep channel-green workspace, dark mud panels, silver-water highlights, subdued marsh grass accents, vertical grass-blade dividers, meandering tidal-channel lines, mudflat texture strips, 8px radius cards, crisp shadcn/tweakcn-style controls, restrained glows, scan-friendly tables.
Required visible sections: title "Salt Marsh Dark", motif story, dark palette swatches, typography with English and Japanese examples, compact left navigation, top navigation, route/tide timeline, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, map/channel panel, data table, form fields, search/command bar, tabs, buttons, badges, alerts, checkbox/radio/switch controls, loading/empty/error status states, spacing/radius/shadow tokens, CSS token snippets, texture strip, generated asset examples.
Palette: deep channel green background, dark wet mud cards, pale silver-water text, muted marsh green primary, reed olive secondary, silt gray borders, tidal blue-green accent, amber wrack warning, clay red destructive only for errors. Avoid pure black.
Text: short readable UI labels only; no long paragraphs. Use generic product UI labels such as Tide Ops, Routes, Berths, Crews, Water Level, Inventory, Dispatch, Checkpoint, Forms, Table, Analytics, Search, Ready, Pending, Alert.
Constraints: no watermark, no random brand names, no unrelated bright colors, no purple gradients, no neon green, no illegible microtext, no marketing hero layout, no animal imagery.
